supervisor_prompt:
Continue from the current repo state in /home/martinkavik/repos/raybox-zig. The primary source of truth is RAYBOX_ZIG_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md. Implement that plan phase by phase until the full final definition of done passes.

Do not discard or overwrite existing user/worktree changes. Preserve the fixed v0 stack exactly: Zig + sokol_app + sokol_gfx + wasm32-emscripten/WebGL2 + Clay + sokol-shdc + CPU 2D geometry + dynamic bitmap glyph atlas. Do not add SDL, raylib, Dear ImGui, Slang, WebGPU, Dawn, wgpu-native, freestanding Wasm, MSDF, 3D modeling, 3D printing, or a general polygon boolean library.

Make reasonable implementation decisions without asking unless genuinely blocked. Follow the plan's non-negotiable rule: no green test may be produced by skipping examples, hiding examples, replacing Boon examples with Zig widgets, ignoring LINK events, returning empty snapshots, mutating corpus_manifest.json into a pass report, or marking unimplemented features as DONE. Unsupported features must fail with explicit diagnostics.

Start with Phase 0. If boon-zig does not expose the required BoonRuntimeHost API, implement that bridge in boon-zig first, pin raybox-zig to the commit containing it, and do not introspect boon-zig private internals or reimplement parser/runtime semantics in raybox-zig.

Keep verification deterministic. Import examples from the pinned Boon playground registry, separate fixtures/corpus_manifest.json from generated zig-out/reports results, use virtual time in expected runners, and implement native/web verifier harnesses as specified. When automation, tooling, verification scripts, or test harnesses are flaky or slowing reliable validation, fix them too.

If the implementation path starts looking too complex, too hacky, circular, or like Codex is spinning in circles, step back, reconsider the plan, and choose a simpler or more general direction that better matches RAYBOX_ZIG_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md instead of forcing a local workaround.

Run relevant checks after each meaningful phase and fix breakage before moving on. Stop only when all final definition-of-done commands pass, or at a real blocker that cannot be resolved locally. If stopping at a blocker, leave the repo coherent and report the exact blocker, evidence, commands run, files changed, and the next concrete step.

Final required commands:
zig build test
zig build verify-corpus
zig build verify-text-native
zig build verify-text-web
zig build verify-examples-native
zig build verify-examples-web
zig build verify-examples-native -Dexample=todo_mvc_physical -Dbuild-only=true
zig build verify-examples-web -Dexample=todo_mvc_physical -Dbuild-only=true
zig build verify-physical-native
zig build verify-physical-web
zig build run-playground
zig build run-playground -Dtarget=wasm32-emscripten
completion_prompt:
Do not implement new functionality yet. Audit the current repo state against RAYBOX_ZIG_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md as the source of truth, tracker, and acceptance reference.

Verify each required outcome against real files, real code, and real command results. Check the fixed stack, version pins, BoonRuntimeHost bridge requirement, corpus import rules, immutable manifest vs generated reports, build command matrix, BUILD.bn host semantics, virtual time, native/web verifier harnesses, text/font behavior, renderer ownership/performance rules, SVG coverage, physical TodoMVC coverage, and final definition of done.

Run the relevant checks and tooling that exist in the repo. Do not claim completion without evidence. Do not accept fake passes caused by skipped examples, empty snapshots, DOM preview rendering, rewritten Zig widgets, ignored LINK events, mutable manifest status, or unimplemented features marked DONE.

If incomplete, produce a concise gap report ordered by implementation phase with file/command evidence and the exact next step. If complete, produce a milestone-by-milestone report with concrete evidence, including the final command results.
